# Soil Health, Fertilizers, Micronutrients
# Generated by KrishiMitra AI Knowledge Base Builder
# Source: ICAR guidelines + agricultural research literature
# Last updated: 2024-25 season

# Comprehensive Soil Health and Fertilizer Guide for Indian Farmers

## Introduction
This guide is designed to assist Indian farmers in managing soil health and optimizing fertilizer application practices. It includes detailed information on soil pH management, reading a Soil Health Card (SHC), recognizing nutrient deficiencies, understanding different types of fertilizers, and applying organic amendments.

---

### 1. Soil pH Scale and Management

- **Soil pH Scale:**
  - <4.5: Very strongly acidic
  - 4.5-5.5: Strongly acidic; apply agricultural lime
  - 5.5-6.0: Moderately acidic; apply lime
  - 6.0-7.5: Optimal range for most crops
  - 7.5-8.0: Moderately alkaline; apply gypsum 2-3 q/acre
  - >8.0: Strongly alkaline; apply gypsum 5 q/acre + pyrite

### 2. Reading a Soil Health Card (SHC)

- **Parameters Tested:** pH, EC, OC, N, P, K, S, Zn, B, Fe, Mn, Cu
- **Website:** [soilhealth.dac.gov.in](http://soilhealth.dac.gov.in)
- **Steps to Get Your SHC Online:**
  - Visit the website.
  - Register and log in if necessary.
  - Enter your field details.
  - Submit a soil sample for testing.

### 3. Nutrient Deficiency Symptoms

- **Nitrogen (N) Deficiency:**
  - Yellow leaves from bottom
  - Apply Urea 46% N at 217 kg/ha per 100 kg N

- **Phosphorus (P) Deficiency:**
  - Purple leaves
  - Apply DAP 18-46-0 at 220 kg/ha per 100 kg P

- **Potassium (K) Deficiency:**
  - Brown leaf margins
  - Apply MOP 60% K at 167 kg/ha per 100 kg K

### 4. Micronutrient Deficiencies and Management

- **Zinc (Zn):**
  - White-yellow stripes in young leaves
  - Apply ZnSO4 21% at 25 kg/ha as basal or chelated Zn foliar 0.5%

- **Boron (B):**
  - Hollow stems, poor pod set
  - Apply Borax Na2B4O7 at 1-2 kg/ha or 0.2% foliar

- **Sulphur (S):**
  - Young leaf yellowing
  - Apply Gypsum at 200 kg/ha or Elemental S at 40 kg/ha — critical for mustard, onion, garlic, pulses

- **Iron (Fe):**
  - Interveinal chlorosis
  - Apply FeSO4 0.5% foliar spray

### 5. Fertilizer Types and Nutrient Content Table

| Fertilizer Type | Nutrient Content |
|----------------|------------------|
| Urea           | 46-0-0            |
| DAP (Di-Ammonium Phosphate) | 18-46-0          |
| SSP (Single Super Phosphate) | 0-16-0 + 12% S   |
| MOP (Mono Ammonium Phosphate) | 0-0-60         |
| NPK            | 10-26-26         |
| NPK            | 20-20-0          |
| NPK            | 12-32-16         |
| Ammonium Sulphate | 20-0-0 + 24% S   |

### 6. Organic Amendments

- **Farmyard Manure (FYM):**
  - 0.5% N applied 10 t/ha
- **Vermicompost:**
  - 1.5-2% N applied 5 t/ha
- **Poultry Manure:**
  - 2-3% N applied 3 t/ha

### 7. How to Collect Soil Sample

- **Steps to Collect Soil Sample:**
  1. Choose a representative field area.
  2. Mark 20 spots in the field for sampling.
  3. Use a soil auger or shovel to collect samples from each spot at a depth of 15 cm.
  4. Mix the collected samples thoroughly.
  5. Air dry the mixed sample and send 500g to your nearest Krishi Vigyan Kendra (KVK).
- **Cost:** Rs 20-50 per test

### 8. Economic Threshold Levels (ETL) for Pests
- ETL values vary by crop and pest, but common thresholds include:
  - Aphids: 10-20 aphids/leaf
  - Whiteflies: 5-10 whiteflies/plant
  - Thrips: 3-5 thrips/cm²

### 9. ICAR/CIB&RC Approved Pesticides
- **Pesticide Name:** Imidacloprid 20% WP (for aphids)
- **Dosage:** 1 kg/ha in 400 liters of water, apply as a foliar spray

### 10. Critical Irrigation Timings
- **Critical Stages for Irrigation:**
  - **Sowing to Germination:** Maintain soil moisture at 75% field capacity.
  - **Vegetative Stage:** Apply irrigation when soil moisture drops below 60% field capacity.
  - **Flowering and Fruiting Stage:** Ensure adequate soil moisture (80-90%) during critical periods.

### 11. Minimum Support Price (MSP) for Crops in 2024-25
- **Cotton:** Rs 7121/q
- **Wheat:** Rs 2425/q
- **Rice:** Rs 2369/q
- **Pulses (Gram):** Rs 5650/q

---

### Conclusion
By following this comprehensive guide, Indian farmers can improve soil health and optimize fertilizer application practices. Regular monitoring of soil parameters and timely intervention will help in achieving higher yields and better economic returns.

For further assistance or specific crop-related queries, contact your local Krishi Vigyan Kendra (KVK) or visit the [Soil Health Card website](http://soilhealth.dac.gov.in).

---

**Note:** Always follow local guidelines and recommendations from agricultural extension services for precise application rates and timings.
